page: Microsoft Azure D32as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dasv7 family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 vCPUs, mapped to 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates exceptional single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top tier. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, falls within the average range. Cryptographic tasks perform strongly, as evidenced by a top 25% PassMark CPU encryption score, aided by hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AES and SHA_NI. Memory performance is also strong, scoring in the top 25% on PassMark memory tests, though uncached large-block read and write bandwidths remain average.

This configuration is highly efficient for workloads requiring strong single-threaded execution and moderate memory density, such as web serving, application hosting, and lightweight database operations. While it excels at small-model LLM inference, achieving top-tier text generation speeds for 135M parameter models, it exhibits a clear performance tradeoff on larger models, with large-model text generation falling into the bottom 10%. Consequently, it is best suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, microservices, and moderate computational tasks rather than heavy deep learning or large-scale parallel processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dasv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks highlight outstanding single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core, single-threaded gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 10%. Multi-core operations and memory bandwidth perform at average levels, while cryptographic tasks and PassMark memory scores rank in the top 25%. This instance is highly suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and small-model language inference, though it faces performance tradeoffs with larger machine learning models.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring a 32-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor and 128 GB RAM. It offers top-tier single-core performance for web serving and general enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v7 is a 32-vCPU general-purpose VM with an AMD EPYC 9V45 CPU and 128 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring high-frequency single-core performance and dedicated AMD EPYC processing for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM.
Top-tier single-core CPU and decompression performance.
Strong cryptographic execution with hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI.
No bundled local storage, but compatible with Premium Storage.
Best suited for web serving, application hosting, and small-model inference.
